title: Dactylorhiza kerryensis
intro: Dactylorhiza kerryensis is a rare, terrestrial orchid species native to Ireland, characterized by its small, white, or pinkish flowers and thick, leathery leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing blood sugar levels and improving cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza kerryensis include iridoids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures, capsules, and tea infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including diabetes and hypertension.
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza kerryensis is a species of orchid in the genus Dactylorhiza. It is a terrestrial orchid and is native to Ireland. The classification of Dactylorhiza kerryensis places it within the family Orchidaceae. This species is often found in wet grasslands and is considered to be a rare and endangered orchid. The classification of Dactylorhiza kerryensis also includes its binomial nomenclature, which is used to identify it as a unique species within the orchid family.
